title: Joby Reports First Quarter 2024 Results
text: Joby Aviation reported Q1 2024 results with significant progress on its electric air taxi development. The company rolled out its second production prototype aircraft and is expanding manufacturing with new facilities in Marina, California and Dayton, Ohio. Joby became the first electric air taxi company to have their final airworthiness criteria published by the FAA. Flight testing has been extensive with the company completing more than 1,500 full-scale eVTOL flights. The company strengthened international presence by signing agreements in Abu Dhabi. Joby also deepened U.S. military ties, committing to deliver two aircraft to MacDill Air Force Base in 2025. The company maintained a strong balance sheet with $924 million in cash and short term investments at quarter-end. Earnings per share were -14 cents, which was above the analyst estimate of -17 cents. Revenue reported was $25,000 with a Q1 net loss of $95 million.
